title: Mars temperature range
content: Surface temperatures on Mars can reach about 20 C near the equator at midday and fall below -125 C near the poles in winter.

title: Mars atmosphere composition
content: Mars has a thin atmosphere made mostly of carbon dioxide, with traces of nitrogen and argon.

title: Length of a sol
content: A Martian day, called a sol, lasts about 24 hours and 37 minutes.

title: Martian year
content: Mars takes about 687 Earth days to complete one orbit around the Sun.

title: Olympus Mons
content: Olympus Mons on Mars is the tallest known volcano in the solar system, rising roughly 22 km above surrounding plains.

title: Valles Marineris
content: Valles Marineris is a canyon system stretching over 4,000 km across Mars and reaching depths of about 7 km.

title: Water evidence on Mars
content: Orbiters and rovers have found mineral and geomorphic evidence that liquid water flowed on ancient Mars.

title: Mars rovers
content: NASA missions such as Spirit, Opportunity, Curiosity, and Perseverance have explored Martian geology and climate.

title: Martian moons
content: Mars has two small moons, Phobos and Deimos, likely captured asteroids.

title: Polar ice caps
content: Mars has polar caps of water ice and seasonal carbon dioxide frost.

title: Dust storms
content: Regional dust storms are common on Mars, and some years they grow into planet-encircling storms.

title: Methane measurements
content: Trace methane detections on Mars are intermittent and remain an active research topic.

title: Sun structure
content: The Sun has a core, radiative zone, convective zone, photosphere, chromosphere, and corona.

title: Solar wind
content: The solar wind is a stream of charged particles flowing outward from the Sun and shaping planetary magnetospheres.

title: Sunspots
content: Sunspots are cooler, darker magnetic regions on the solar surface.

title: Solar cycle
content: Sunspot activity rises and falls in an approximately 11-year solar cycle.

title: Nuclear fusion in the Sun
content: The Sun produces energy through nuclear fusion that converts hydrogen into helium in its core.

title: Coronal mass ejections
content: Coronal mass ejections can send large plasma clouds into space and trigger geomagnetic storms at Earth.

title: Mercury temperature extremes
content: Mercury's near-airless surface experiences daytime temperatures above 400 C and nighttime lows near -180 C.

title: Mercury orbit
content: Mercury is the closest planet to the Sun and completes an orbit in about 88 Earth days.

title: Mercury geology
content: Mercury has heavily cratered terrain, smooth plains, and large lobate scarps from planetary contraction.

title: MESSENGER mission
content: NASA's MESSENGER orbited Mercury from 2011 to 2015 and mapped its surface and composition.

title: Mercury resonance
content: Mercury rotates three times for every two orbits, a 3:2 spin-orbit resonance.

title: Venus atmosphere
content: Venus has a dense carbon dioxide atmosphere with thick sulfuric acid clouds.

title: Runaway greenhouse effect
content: A strong greenhouse effect makes Venus the hottest planetary surface in the solar system.

title: Venus surface conditions
content: Surface pressure on Venus is about 92 times Earth's sea-level pressure.

title: Retrograde rotation
content: Venus rotates slowly in a retrograde direction, so the Sun appears to rise in the west there.

title: Volcanic plains
content: Radar observations show Venus has broad volcanic plains and many shield volcanoes.

title: Earth atmosphere
content: Earth's atmosphere is mostly nitrogen and oxygen and supports a stable water cycle.

title: Earth magnetic field
content: Earth's magnetic field helps shield the planet from solar wind particles.

title: Earth and the Moon
content: The Moon stabilizes Earth's axial tilt and influences ocean tides.

title: Water cycle
content: Solar heating drives evaporation, condensation, precipitation, and runoff in Earth's water cycle.

title: Plate tectonics
content: Earth is the only known planet with active global plate tectonics.

title: Habitable conditions
content: Earth's distance from the Sun allows long-term liquid water at the surface.

title: Great Red Spot
content: Jupiter's Great Red Spot is a giant, long-lived anticyclonic storm larger than Earth.

title: Jupiter atmosphere
content: Jupiter's atmosphere is mainly hydrogen and helium with bands shaped by strong winds.

title: Io volcanism
content: Io is the most volcanically active moon in the solar system due to tidal heating.

title: Europa ocean
content: Europa likely has a global subsurface ocean beneath an icy crust.

title: Ganymede magnetism
content: Ganymede is the largest moon and the only moon known to have its own intrinsic magnetic field.

title: Callisto surface
content: Callisto has an ancient, heavily cratered surface with relatively low geological activity.

title: Jupiter magnetosphere
content: Jupiter has the largest and most powerful planetary magnetosphere in the solar system.

title: Jupiter rings
content: Jupiter has faint dusty rings discovered by the Voyager missions.

title: Jupiter interior
content: Models indicate Jupiter likely has a dense core surrounded by metallic hydrogen and molecular hydrogen layers.

title: Juno mission
content: NASA's Juno mission studies Jupiter's gravity, magnetic field, atmosphere, and interior structure.

title: Saturn rings
content: Saturn's bright rings are made mostly of water-ice particles ranging from dust to boulder size.

title: Saturn atmosphere
content: Saturn's atmosphere is primarily hydrogen and helium with high-speed zonal winds.

title: Titan atmosphere
content: Titan has a thick nitrogen-rich atmosphere and methane-ethane weather cycles.

title: Enceladus plumes
content: Enceladus ejects water-rich plumes from its south polar region, indicating a subsurface ocean.

title: Cassini mission
content: The Cassini spacecraft orbited Saturn from 2004 to 2017 and transformed understanding of the system.

title: Saturn density
content: Saturn's average density is lower than water, about 0.69 g/cm3.

title: Hexagon storm
content: A persistent hexagonal jet stream circles Saturn's north pole.

title: Ring age debate
content: Scientists continue to study whether Saturn's rings are ancient or comparatively young.

title: Uranus axial tilt
content: Uranus rotates on its side with an axial tilt of about 98 degrees.

title: Uranus atmosphere
content: Uranus has a hydrogen-helium atmosphere with methane that gives the planet a cyan color.

title: Uranus rings
content: Uranus has a set of narrow, dark rings.

title: Uranian moons
content: Major moons of Uranus include Titania, Oberon, Umbriel, Ariel, and Miranda.

title: Seasonal extremes
content: Because of its tilt, Uranus experiences extreme seasonal illumination patterns.

title: Neptune atmosphere
content: Neptune's atmosphere contains hydrogen, helium, and methane with dynamic cloud systems.

title: Great Dark Spot
content: Voyager 2 observed a large storm called the Great Dark Spot in Neptune's atmosphere.

title: Triton
content: Triton orbits Neptune in a retrograde direction, suggesting it was captured from the Kuiper Belt.

title: Neptune winds
content: Neptune hosts some of the fastest winds measured in the solar system, exceeding 1,500 km/h.

title: Neptune ring arcs
content: Neptune has faint rings including clumpy arcs stabilized by gravitational interactions.

title: Pluto reclassification
content: Pluto was reclassified as a dwarf planet in 2006 under the IAU definition.

title: Ceres location
content: Ceres is a dwarf planet in the main asteroid belt between Mars and Jupiter.

title: Eris discovery
content: The discovery of Eris helped motivate the modern dwarf-planet category.

title: Haumea shape
content: Haumea has an elongated shape and rotates rapidly.

title: Makemake
content: Makemake is a Kuiper Belt dwarf planet with a very cold, methane-rich surface.

title: New Horizons at Pluto
content: NASA's New Horizons flew past Pluto in 2015 and revealed diverse terrains including Sputnik Planitia.

title: Pluto atmosphere
content: Pluto has a thin nitrogen atmosphere that expands and contracts with its seasonal cycle.

title: Dwarf planet criteria
content: A dwarf planet orbits the Sun and is nearly round but has not cleared its orbital neighborhood.

title: Asteroid belt
content: The main asteroid belt lies between Mars and Jupiter and contains rocky bodies of many sizes.

title: Comets
content: Comets are icy bodies that form comae and tails when heated near the Sun.

title: Kuiper Belt
content: The Kuiper Belt is a disk of icy objects beyond Neptune.

title: Oort Cloud
content: The Oort Cloud is a distant spherical reservoir thought to supply many long-period comets.

title: Meteoroids
content: Meteoroids are small space rocks; meteors are the streaks of light when they enter an atmosphere.

title: Near-Earth asteroids
content: Near-Earth asteroids have orbits that bring them close to Earth's orbital path.

title: Planetary defense
content: Planetary defense programs track potentially hazardous asteroids and test deflection methods.

title: Asteroid samples
content: Sample-return missions like Hayabusa2 and OSIRIS-REx provide direct data on asteroid composition.

title: Solar system formation
content: The solar system formed about 4.6 billion years ago from a rotating cloud of gas and dust.

title: Protoplanetary disk
content: Planets formed within a protoplanetary disk as dust grains collided and accreted into larger bodies.

title: Inner vs outer planets
content: Rocky terrestrial planets dominate the inner solar system, while giant planets dominate farther out.

title: Frost line
content: Beyond the frost line, volatile ices could condense, helping giant-planet cores grow quickly.

title: Habitable zone
content: The habitable zone is the orbital range where liquid water could persist on a suitable planetary surface.

title: Astronomical unit
content: One astronomical unit is the average Earth-Sun distance, about 149.6 million km.

title: Orbital eccentricity
content: Eccentricity describes how circular or elongated an orbit is.

title: Inclination
content: Orbital inclination measures tilt relative to a reference plane, often the ecliptic.

title: Resonances
content: Orbital resonances occur when bodies have orbital periods with simple integer ratios.

title: Tidal forces
content: Tidal forces can heat interiors, lock rotations, and reshape orbits over time.

title: Space age milestones
content: Early space milestones include Sputnik, human orbital flight, and lunar landings.

title: Voyager missions
content: Voyager 1 and 2 explored outer planets and now travel through interstellar space.

title: Pioneer missions
content: Pioneer probes provided early flyby data for Jupiter and Saturn.

title: Mariner program
content: Mariner missions returned foundational observations of Mercury, Venus, and Mars.

title: Comparative planetology
content: Comparative planetology studies similarities and differences among worlds to explain planetary evolution.

title: Planetary atmospheres
content: Atmospheres evolve through volcanic outgassing, impacts, chemistry, and atmospheric escape.

title: Magnetospheres
content: Planetary magnetospheres interact with solar wind and can protect atmospheres from erosion.

title: Cratering record
content: Impact craters preserve a timeline of bombardment, especially on airless or geologically quiet surfaces.

title: Exoplanet context
content: Exoplanet discoveries help place our solar system in a broader galactic context.

title: Transit method
content: The transit method detects exoplanets by measuring periodic dips in stellar brightness.

title: Direct imaging
content: Direct imaging reveals some giant exoplanets and circumstellar disks around nearby stars.

title: Future exploration
content: Future missions target ocean worlds, sample return, and improved planetary defense capabilities.
